key_metrics.claim: Organic revenue growth of 3-5% is considered strong for household products companies, reflecting pricing power and volume gains.

valuation_approach.claim: Household products companies typically trade at 20-25x forward P/E due to predictable cash flows and defensive characteristics.

regulatory_landscape.claim: FDA regulation of cleaning product ingredients and labeling requirements create compliance costs but also barriers to entry.

historical_precedents.claim: Procter & Gamble's 2014-2016 brand portfolio restructuring shed 100+ brands to focus on 65 core brands, improving margins by 200bps.
historical_precedents.source_title: Harvard Business Review - P&G Restructuring Case Study

moat_sources.claim: Brand loyalty and shelf-space dominance in retail channels create durable competitive advantages for established household products companies.

kill_factors.claim: Private label penetration above 40% in a category signals permanent margin compression and brand commoditization.

fcf_margin_ranges.claim: Best-in-class household products companies generate 15-20% FCF margins; average performers deliver 8-12%.

typical_multiples.claim: Household products companies typically trade at 15-20x EV/EBITDA and 22-28x P/FCF for premium franchises.
typical_multiples.source_title: Goldman Sachs Consumer Staples Valuation Guide
